display_name: Filtering ICMPv6 breaks IPv6 Neighbor Discovery and PMTUD — it is not just 'blocking ping'
context: After an IPv6 ACL change, neighbors stop resolving, hosts lose their gateway, or large flows hang, and the symptoms look like a link or routing problem
observation: Neighbors flip to INCOMPLETE and/or large transfers stall right after an ICMPv6/IPv6 ACL was applied; removing the ACL restores connectivity
naive_interpretation: There is an L2/link problem or an MTU/routing issue on the path
correct_interpretation: Operators frequently port an IPv4 'deny icmp' ACL to IPv6 without realizing that IPv6 Neighbor Discovery itself is ICMPv6. Dropping ICMPv6 types 133-136 kills router and neighbor discovery — NS/NA never complete, RS/RA never flow — so neighbors go INCOMPLETE and hosts lose their default gateway. Separately, filtering ICMPv6 type 2 (Packet Too Big) silently breaks IPv6 Path MTU Discovery: small packets pass while large flows black-hole, which mimics an intermittent or application-layer problem. The correlation tell is that connectivity changed exactly when an ACL/CoPP policy changed. The fix is to explicitly permit ND types 133,134,135,136 (and 137 if redirects are used) and type 2, plus link-local (fe80::/10) and solicited-node multicast (ff02::1:ff00:0/104), before any ICMPv6 deny.
why: ND is carried in ICMPv6, so an ICMPv6 deny removes the control messages that resolve neighbors and discover routers
IPv6 has no in-network fragmentation; PMTUD relies entirely on ICMPv6 Packet Too Big, so filtering type 2 black-holes large packets
The failure correlates tightly with the ACL/CoPP change time, distinguishing it from a real link or routing fault

confirmation_commands.purpose: Check for ICMPv6 deny entries and rising hit counters correlated with ND failures
Inspect permit/deny lines for ICMPv6 types and their counters
Inspect ICMPv6 entries and hardware/software hit counts
Look for rules matching ICMPv6 (IP protocol 58) and their action

traps: Treating an ICMPv6 deny as merely 'blocking ping' when it also kills ND
Permitting Echo (128/129) but forgetting ND types 133-136, so ping works in tests but real resolution fails
Filtering type 2 Packet Too Big and then chasing a phantom MTU/application bug
Not permitting link-local and solicited-node multicast that ND requires

sources.title: RFC 4861 — Neighbor Discovery for IPv6 (neighbor cache states 7.3.2)
